lead_profile_text: 10-year property investor (buy, renovate, rent) concluding BTL margins are insufficient and pivoting to STL. Kinver property (3-bed semi at 6 Jenks Avenue) mid-renovation — ~5-6 weeks from completion. Plans to sell property into his limited company, refinance, and use equity to acquire a second property in the village (detached riverside cottage already identified). Partner has hotel housekeeping background, 5 min from property — will handle changeovers as invoiced contractor, saving ~£10,958/year in cleaning costs. Noted 12% management fee discount at 3+ properties as relevant.
emotional_profile: Commercially sophisticated and portfolio-minded. 'This is potentially the start to something a lot bigger.' Wants data-driven reassurance on winter occupancy and long-term model viability. Prefers phone and email over Slack. Needs 3-year revenue projection for bank refinancing — a hard commitment from Zac.
delay_trigger_phrases: No specific follow-up meeting date agreed
Zac to arrange property visit once renovation nears completion
delay_timeline: Renovation ~5-6 weeks from completion as of 2026-06-26. Sofiane to send photos/video; Zac to prepare 3-year projection after receiving photos; property visit arranged once renovation nears completion.
green_light_trigger_phrases: This is potentially the start to something a lot bigger
If this works, I'm actually converting the riverside cottage next
Can you do a business plan — three years, your expectation for the first year, second year, third year

top_objections.text: Winter occupancy / non-leisure demand: how does Stayful fill the property in January-March without leisure travellers?
top_objections.zac_response: Corporate/contractor demand from Kidderminster, Stourbridge, and Wolverhampton within commuting distance; low local competition means fewer alternatives for contractors

top_objections.text: Ramp-up period: wants realistic expectations on time to top 10% ranking
top_objections.zac_response: 7-10 months given low local competition; Sofiane accepted this framing

top_objections.text: Airbnb fee confusion: heard Airbnb is moving to 15% host-only fee; worried how that interacts with 15% management fee
top_objections.zac_response: Clarified context and how Stayful's 15% management fee relates to platform fees independently

top_objections.text: Direct bookings: strong preference for reducing platform dependency
top_objections.zac_response: Stayful operates its own direct booking site — had not known this; resonated strongly

top_objections.text: Communication preference: does not want to use Slack; prefers phone and email
top_objections.zac_response: Partially resolved on the call — communication preference noted

top_objections.text: Management fee flexibility: asked about discounts
top_objections.zac_response: 12%+VAT available at 3+ properties — relevant to his expansion plan

top_questions.text: How does Stayful fill the property in winter without leisure demand?
top_questions.answer: Corporate and contractor demand from Kidderminster, Stourbridge, and Wolverhampton within commuting distance. Low local competition in Kinver means few STL alternatives for contractors needing nearby accommodation.

top_questions.text: Is there a management fee discount for multiple properties?
top_questions.answer: Yes — 12%+VAT at 3+ properties (vs standard 15%+VAT). Relevant to Sofiane's plan to convert the riverside cottage and potentially a third property.

top_questions.text: Can you provide a 3-year revenue projection for bank refinancing?
top_questions.answer: Yes — Zac to prepare 3-year business plan after receiving property photos/video. This is needed for Sofiane's limited company refinancing.

what_moved_them: Partner handling changeovers — immediately understood the financial significance (~£12,000 additional to household income annually)
Direct booking capability through Stayful's own site — had not known Stayful operated one; resonated strongly
Low local competition in Kinver (high house prices limiting supply) — confirmed his own observation
Manchester 3-bed comparable (projected £62k, achieved £56k — net came in higher due to direct bookings)
Portfolio pathway — 12% management fee at 3+ properties is directly relevant to his expansion vision
what_fell_flat: Slack as primary communication channel — Sofiane does not want to use Slack; prefers phone and email

conversion_gaps.gap: 3-year revenue projection needed for bank refinancing before Sofiane can proceed
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Zac to prepare 3-year business plan after receiving photos/video from Sofiane. This is a hard commitment and a strong conversion tool.

conversion_gaps.gap: Renovation not complete — property not ready to list for ~5-6 weeks
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Use the 5-6 week window to complete 3-year projection, arrange property visit, and confirm furnishing/setup plan so onboarding is immediate once renovation completes
Accommodate phone and email as primary; offer Slack as optional rather than mandatory

offer_framing: 12%+VAT at 3+ properties noted but no formal offer made
zac_income_intro: Annual gross ~£60,876, net ~£31,652 (£2,638/month) at £277 ADR and 57% occupancy. Partner handling changeovers saves ~£10,958/year — effectively lifting net to ~£42,600/year at full run-rate. Long-let comparison: £1,026/month (£12,312/year).
zac_transition_phrases: I think we're in phase on that end
zac_reassurance_phrases: Your partner doing changeovers is genuinely a structural advantage — that's ~£12,000 more in your pocket every year
We have our own direct booking site — repeat guests and referrals go through us, not through Airbnb
Kinver has high house prices which limits supply — there aren't many STL properties for contractors to choose from
zac_analogies: Manchester 3-bed comparable: projected £62k gross, achieved £56k gross — but net came in higher due to direct bookings reducing platform commission
